subject: [PATCH 4/5] iova: alignment-aware two-phase search for log(n) aligned alloc

2026-05-13.patch_summary: This patch improves the performance of IOMMU allocation by introducing a two-phase search algorithm in the __iova_search_free_gap function. The first phase prunes the tree based on alignment-aware bounds, reducing the number of nodes to visit and making the search O(log n). If this phase fails, the second phase falls back to the existing behavior, which is O(n) but only triggers when no suitable gap exists in the tree. This approach ensures that allocations are aligned correctly while minimizing the number of nodes visited during the search process.
